howItWorks.intro: This battery backup duration calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Usable Energy = Battery kWh x Usable % x Inverter Efficiency %
howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How long will a Tesla Powerwall last in an outage?
faq.answer: A single Powerwall (13.5 kWh) lasts 10-12 hours powering essential loads (fridge, lights, WiFi, phones = ~1.2 kW), or 4-5 hours with heavier loads including AC. Two Powerwalls can typically get through a full day.

faq.question: What should I power during an outage?
faq.answer: Priority loads: refrigerator (0.15 kW), lights (0.1 kW), WiFi router (0.02 kW), phone charging (0.01 kW). Total essentials ~0.5 kW. Adding a well pump (1 kW), sump pump (0.5 kW), or AC (3 kW) drastically reduces runtime.

faq.question: Is the Battery Backup Duration Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What battery capacity should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate battery capacity value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Battery Backup Duration Calculator?
sections.body: The Battery Backup Duration Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your battery capacity, essential load, usable capacity, you get instant results including usable energy, backup runtime, full days of backup. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting usable energy right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your battery capacity and need to find the right usable energy. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Battery Capacity (kWh)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your battery capacity — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: essential load, usable capacity, inverter efficiency. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your usable energy, backup runtime, full days of backup. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your battery capacity before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both usable energy and backup runtime — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your inverter efficiency, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
